title: Team
text:
The SEEC leadership team decided to present our members in three groups: leadership team, rest of the core team and affiliates. We used the below guidelines on what is expected of the different categories of affiliation to decide who should be a core member vs affiliate. This was circulated to the all SEEC members for feedback and adjusted on the website accordingly. The curren team and affiliations can be seen in Table @tab:affaca.
SEEC Academics and Postdoctoral Fellows (Core Team)

These are people who view the success of SEEC and their own academic success as being connected to some degree.
They attend the monthly SEEC staff meetings as this is where the direction of SEEC is developed.
Take a leadership role or assist with the organising of the various facets of SEEC e.g. Stats Toolbox, research seminars.
Make an effort to contribute to raising funds to support SEEC core activities and shared resources.
Benefit from shared SEEC resources (admin support, computers, office space).
Contribute ideas when called upon to do so.
Attend the annual SEEC Student symposium if at all possible.
Make every reasonable effort to attend research meetings.
List SEEC as an affiliation on any papers written or presentations given.
Contribute to the SEEC annual report.
Actively collaborate with other SEEC members.
If not based at a South African University, SEEC core members need to be Honorary Research Associates.
Are on the seec-staff-l mailing list.


SEEC Postgraduates

These are students whose research project and interests fall within the parameters of Statistics in Ecology, Environment and Conservation and whose supervisor is supportive of the connection to the group. They have a (co)supervisor who is part of the core group or an Affiliated Academic.
Present at least two talks each year. One will be at the annual SEEC symposium and the other will be at the research meetings held at three-weekly intervals.
Attend the annual SEEC Student symposium.
Attend all research meetings (arrangements can be made for SEEC students who are not based in Cape Town).
List SEEC as an affiliation on any papers written or presentations given.
Are on the seec-all-l mailing list.

SEEC Affiliated Academic

These academics are SEEC members but not willing or able to contribute to the same degree as a core team member, either because of commitments with other research groups or because their area of interest only overlaps partly with SEEC’s vision.
Add their SEEC affiliation to their publications where appropriate.
Willing to work with the group, contribute to meetings and joint projects, bring opportunities to the attention of the group and promote the group in conversations if the opportunity arises.
Can benefit from shared SEEC resources if they contribute funding.
Postgraduate students of affiliated academics can be SEEC postgraduate students.
Can be SEEC Honorary Research Associates if not based at a South African university.
Attend the SEEC student symposium and research meetings if possible.
Are on the seec-staff-l mailing list.

SEEC Undergraduates

Undergraduate students who are interested in the work we do at SEEC.
They can be involved in research projects.
They are encouraged to participate in the Research Meetings and to attend the student symposium.
They can be mentored by SEEC Postgraduates or academics.
Are on the seec-all-l mailing list.

SEEC Advisory Board

Attend and give inputs at board meetings.
Give inputs and advice when called upon.
Are being updated about SEEC activities through the annual report and at meetings.

SEEC Partners

Academics or scientists working at a university or research organisation who collaborate with the SEEC core team, be it as co-supervisors, or collaborators on projects and funding applications.
These academics are not formally part of the SEEC team and are not expected to list SEEC as an affiliation on papers and presentations.
Can be consulted for inputs on SEEC strategy or projects.
Can be on the the seec-friends-l mailing list.

SEEC Friends

Anyone who is interested in what we do and would like to be informed of seminars (Stats Toolbox) and courses.
There is no obligation or commitment either on part of SEEC or the Friends.
Are on the seec-friends-l mailing list or keep themselves in the loop through the SEEC facebook page, twitter or web site.

Last updated: 2026-01-21 12:03 SAST

title: Home
text:
Last updated: r format(updated, “%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z”)
This site presents the SEEC 2025 Annual Report as a live, web-based document.
All tables are generated directly from the SEEC Google Sheets and update automatically whenever the site is rebuilt.

title: Students
text:
Summary table of registered students
This table summarises the number of currently registered SEEC postgraduate students, broken down by first year of registration and degree type. It provides an overview of cohort sizes over time and highlights the current balance between PhD and Masters students.

title: Students
text:
Summary table of graduated students
This table summarises SEEC postgraduate students who have graduated, grouped by graduation year and degree type. It provides an overview of student throughput and completion patterns over recent years.

title: Students

This table summarises SEEC postdoctoral researchers, grouped by year and host department. It provides an overview of postdoctoral involvement across departments and time.
